summary.what_happened: Logins fail intermittently on SSO token-exchange timeouts from about 07:18.
summary.customer_impact: Some users cannot log in on first attempt.

incident_window.rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-15T07:40:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; no metrics available to detect recovery -> ongoing

severity.explanation: login failures are confirmed by logs but no metrics are in the package, so failure rate and user share cannot be quantified - SEV-3 as limited-degradation floor, low confidence

timeline.description: ERROR: SSO token exchange timed out after 3000ms
ERROR: SSO token exchange timed out after 3000ms
ERROR: login failed for session 4471: upstream sso timeout
Alert fired: Login failure rate elevated on auth-service
ERROR: login failed for session 4519: upstream sso timeout
WARN: retrying token exchange with backoff (attempt 2)

evidence.interpretation: deploys.json is an empty list: change window checked, nothing shipped - internal change ruled out
SSO token exchange degrades from slow (1900ms, 07:18) to timeouts (3000ms) causing login failures

hypotheses.title: sso-provider degradation timing out token exchanges
hypotheses.statement: Log timeouts against the external SSO provider are consistent with upstream degradation, but with no metrics the scale and onset cannot be corroborated - single-source evidence only.
assumptions: log sampling did not hide earlier symptoms
recommended_checks: check the SSO provider status page and error rates from a probe
pull auth-service success-rate metrics from the source system
